creator: rkoberman@gmail.com
text:
I just hit this on 10.2-STABLE (r297164). Built with the following options with both portmaster and simply running 'make' in the directory with a clean ports tree, running as root, and llvm36 installed. Here are my options:
===> The following configuration options are available for rust-1.7.0:
     GDB=off: Install ports gdb (necessary for debugging rust programs)
     LLNEXTGEN=off: Build with grammar verification
     PORT_LLVM=on: Build against devel/llvm36 instead of bundled version
===> Use 'make config' to modify these settings


This is happening during staging after the port builds correctly. I'm unsure where the working directory might be at the time. Here is a list of the full staging attempt:
make stage
===>  Staging for rust-1.7.0
===>   rust-1.7.0 depends on shared library: libedit.so.0 - found (/usr/local/lib/libedit.so.0)
===>   Generating temporary packing list
gmake[2]: Entering directory '/usr/ports/lang/rust/work/rustc-1.7.0'
cfg: version 1.7.0
cfg: build triple x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: host triples x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: target triples x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: host for x86_64-unknown-freebsd is x86_64
cfg: os for x86_64-unknown-freebsd is unknown-freebsd
cfg: no good valgrind for x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: disabling valgrind run-pass tests
cfg: disabling unstable features (CFG_DISABLE_UNSTABLE_FEATURES)
cfg: disabling doc build (CFG_DISABLE_DOCS)
cfg: including prepare rules
cfg: including dist rules
cfg: including install rules
cfg: version 1.7.0
cfg: build triple x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: host triples x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: target triples x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: host for x86_64-unknown-freebsd is x86_64
cfg: os for x86_64-unknown-freebsd is unknown-freebsd
cfg: no good valgrind for x86_64-unknown-freebsd
cfg: using CC=cc (CFG_CC)
cfg: using CXX=c++ (CFG_CXX)
cfg: using CPP=cpp (CFG_CPP)
cfg: using CFLAGS=-O2 -pipe  -isystem /usr/local/include -fstack-protector -fno-strict-aliasing (CFG_CFLAGS)
cfg: using CXXFLAGS=-O2 -pipe -isystem /usr/local/include -fstack-protector -fno-strict-aliasing  -isystem /usr/local/include (CFG_CXXFLAGS)
cfg: disabling valgrind run-pass tests
cfg: disabling unstable features (CFG_DISABLE_UNSTABLE_FEATURES)
cfg: disabling doc build (CFG_DISABLE_DOCS)
cfg: including prepare rules
cfg: including dist rules
cfg: including install rules
cleaning destination tmp/dist/rustc-1.7.0-x86_64-unknown-freebsd-image
prepare: tmp/dist/rustc-1.7.0-x86_64-unknown-freebsd-image/bin
mkdir: tmp/dist: Permission denied
/usr/ports/lang/rust/work/rustc-1.7.0/mk/dist.mk:126: recipe for target 'prepare-host-dirs-dir-x86_64-unknown-freebsd' failed
gmake: *** [prepare-host-dirs-dir-x86_64-unknown-freebsd] Error 1
/usr/ports/lang/rust/work/rustc-1.7.0/mk/install.mk:20: recipe for target 'install' failed
gmake[2]: *** [install] Error 2
gmake[2]: Leaving directory '/usr/ports/lang/rust/work/rustc-1.7.0'
*** Error code 2

Stop.
make[1]: stopped in /usr/ports/lang/rust
*** Error code 1

This is now blocking hte upgrade of firefox-45, though I can turn off the rust option, now. I understand that rust will be required in the future, though that is just hear-say.
